title: Living in Boulder vlog 7 -  22 -  2018: Extracting numbers from History Books

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a life update from his Boulder apartment, covering his progress editing 264 history books on his website by removing footnote reference numbers and adding chapter numbers, his new two-computer workflow, and a room reorganization where he moved his bed to the floor to work lying down watching a wall-mounted TV. He also briefly mentions a troll video made from out-of-context clips of him.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: removing footnote numbers from history books
stances.position: necessary but tedious; can't batch-automate because numbers are also dates and chapter markers
more comfortable and productive; dismisses commenter's concern that it's unhealthy
flagged and disliked it; plans a response page but expects it to be taken down before he gets to it
wants to do it but it will take months; planning to do only the interesting/easy stuff

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been living in Boulder for about a week since my last video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The last time I did a video, I was about to put images on my history books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I added 249 photos to the strawberry chapter, which is 1800 pages long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Chapter eight of the strawberry book has a lot of photos, giving a good sample of what's coming.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I realized the history books have too many numbers from sources like archive.org, Wiki source, and Project Gutenberg.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The numbers in the books are annoying because they take me out of the reading experience.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to remove all the numbers from my pension history books on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't batch edit the numbers because some are dates or chapter numbers, so I have to remove them manually.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought my second computer was arriving the day I last made a video, but it actually came a few days later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The new computer is better than the old one, though it still has some waiting issues.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm editing the books by adding chapter numbers and removing unnecessary numbers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have 264 books to edit, totaling around 170,000 pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The average book length is about 650 pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After editing, I plan to fix the links and then attach images to the books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might start with Thucydides' 'History of the Peloponnesian War' for adding images.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I reorganized my room on a whim, moving my bed to the floor in front of the drawers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I now work lying down with a magic keyboard, which feels much more comfortable.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Someone commented that my lifestyle is unhealthy, but I feel better working this way.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I saw a troll video that took my words out of context, so I flagged and disliked it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might make a response video if the troll video is still up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently working on manually removing page numbers from a 3200-page book.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My old computer crashed multiple times while editing, so I switched to the new one.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm now working on another 1500-page book, manually removing numbers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to check back in a week, hoping to finish extracting and adding numbers to all the books.
